title: ancistrachne numaeensis
intro: Ancistrachne numaeensis, a rare and endemic species of grass, is a type of plant native to New Zealand. The medicinal properties of Ancistrachne numaeensis have been traditionally used by the indigenous Maori people, who valued its ability to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of this plant include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of skin conditions and infections. Herbal preparations of Ancistrachne numaeensis can be made into teas, infusions, or topical ointments, although further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal applications.
